title: Worker Pool for Fuzzy Search
impact: 2-4x speedup for fuzzy search on large graphs by parallelizing Levenshtein distance calculations

tasks.description: Create Levenshtein Worker Module - Create src/workers/levenshteinWorker.ts that calculates Levenshtein distances in a worker thread
stepByStep: Create directory: src/workers/
Create new file: src/workers/levenshteinWorker.ts
Add import: import { parentPort, workerData } from 'worker_threads';
Define WorkerInput interface with query (string), entities (array), threshold (number)
Define MatchResult interface with name, score, matchedIn
Implement levenshteinDistance function using dynamic programming matrix
Implement similarity function: 1 - (distance / max(len1, len2))
Add main worker logic: if (parentPort) { ... }
Destructure workerData as WorkerInput
Iterate entities, calculate name similarity, if >= threshold add to results
If name doesn't match, check observations
parentPort.postMessage(results) at the end
Run: npm run typecheck
acceptanceCriteria: Worker receives workerData and posts results

tasks.description: Create WorkerPool Manager - Create src/workers/WorkerPool.ts that manages a pool of worker threads
stepByStep: Create new file: src/workers/WorkerPool.ts
Add all imports
Define WorkerTask interface with data, resolve, reject
Define PoolOptions interface with optional maxWorkers and required workerPath
Create WorkerPool class with private workers array, taskQueue array, activeWorkers counter
Implement constructor: set maxWorkers to options.maxWorkers ?? cpus().length - 1, store workerPath
Implement execute(data): create Promise, push task to queue, call processQueue, return promise
Implement executeAll(items): map items to execute calls, return Promise.all
Implement processQueue: while queue has items AND activeWorkers < maxWorkers, shift task and runTask
Implement runTask: increment activeWorkers, create Worker with workerData, set up message/error/exit handlers
On message: resolve task, decrement activeWorkers, terminate worker, processQueue
On error: reject task, decrement activeWorkers, terminate worker, processQueue
Implement shutdown: wait loop until activeWorkers is 0
Implement getStats: return { activeWorkers, queueSize, maxWorkers }
Create src/workers/index.ts with: export { WorkerPool } from './WorkerPool.js';
Run: npm run typecheck
acceptanceCriteria: Pool limits concurrent workers to maxWorkers
Tasks queued when all workers busy

tasks.description: Integrate Worker Pool into FuzzySearch - Modify FuzzySearch to use worker pool for large graphs with low thresholds
stepByStep: Open src/search/FuzzySearch.ts
Add imports: import { WorkerPool } from '../workers/WorkerPool.js';
Add imports: import { fileURLToPath } from 'url'; import { dirname, join } from 'path';
Add constants after imports: const WORKER_MIN_ENTITIES = 500; const WORKER_MAX_THRESHOLD = 0.8;
Add private property: private workerPool: WorkerPool<any, any> | null = null;
Add private property: private workerPath: string;
In constructor (or add one if needed): calculate workerPath using fileURLToPath and dirname
In fuzzySearch method: after getting entities from loadGraph, check if shouldUseWorkers
shouldUseWorkers = entities.length >= WORKER_MIN_ENTITIES && threshold < WORKER_MAX_THRESHOLD
If shouldUseWorkers, call searchWithWorkers and return result
Implement searchWithWorkers method:
- Initialize workerPool if null
- Split entities into chunks (e.g., 4 chunks)
- Create workerInputs array with query, threshold, entities (with lowercased fields)
- Call workerPool.executeAll(workerInputs)
- Flatten results, create Set of matched names, filter entities
Implement shutdown method to clean up worker pool
Run: npm run typecheck
acceptanceCriteria: FuzzySearch uses workers for >= 500 entities with threshold < 0.8
Worker pool only initialized when needed
shutdown() method cleans up worker pool

tasks.description: Add Worker Pool Tests - Create tests/unit/workers/WorkerPool.test.ts with unit tests
stepByStep: Create directory: tests/unit/workers/
Create new file: tests/unit/workers/WorkerPool.test.ts
Add all imports
Create describe('WorkerPool') block
Test 1: 'should report correct initial stats' - create pool with maxWorkers: 2, verify stats
Test 2: 'should respect maxWorkers limit' - verify activeWorkers never exceeds max
Test 3: 'should queue tasks when workers busy' - verify queueSize increases
Note: Full worker execution tests may require building the worker first
Add describe('FuzzySearch with Workers') with placeholder tests for integration
Run: npx vitest run tests/unit/workers/

successCriteria: Levenshtein worker module created and compiles
WorkerPool manager created with task queuing
FuzzySearch uses workers for large/low-threshold searches
notes: This sprint adds parallel fuzzy search for large graphs. The worker pool spawns workers per-task to avoid memory leaks. Workers are only used when beneficial (large graph + low threshold). Can run in parallel with Sprint 2 after Sprint 1 completes. IMPORTANT: The main fuzzy search method is named 'fuzzySearch()' not 'search()'. The FuzzySearch class already has a constructor that takes GraphStorage - the workerPath calculation should be added to it. Workers require the compiled .js files in dist/, so ensure 'npm run build' completes before testing workers.
